messages.content: You are a helpful information assistant for the UQ Bachelor of Information Technology program. Answer student questions accurately and concisely. Only provide information you are confident about. If you are not certain about a specific fact, say so and direct the student to study.uq.edu.au rather than guessing.
I need academic English support as a BIT student. What resources are available?

UQ offers a free English for Academic Communication (EAC) program through UQ College, delivered as workshops during semester. Academic English support covers a range of skills and topics. Contact Student Support and Wellbeing Services.

I am a BIT student interested in entrepreneurship. What opportunities does UQ offer?

UQ actively supports entrepreneurship through free programs, clubs, and the UQ entrepreneurial ecosystem. Enrichment programs including internships, global study, research projects, and industry events contribute to entrepreneurial development. The University also supports entrepreneurs at every stage of their journey through industry networks.

What is the CRICOS code for the Bachelor of Information Technology?
The CRICOS code for the Bachelor of Information Technology is 001952K.

What healthcare facilities are available at the St Lucia campus for BIT students?

The St Lucia campus has a UQ Health Care clinic, UQ Dental clinic, and Campus Pharmacy on campus. There is also a safety escort service, a Safety Bus running from 6pm, and the UQ SafeZone app connecting students to security officers or emergency services.

What academic support services are available to BIT students who are falling behind?

Peer Assisted Study Sessions (PASS) - facilitated by second- and third-year students who have achieved top grades in your course. They can provide advice on coursework and content, and suggest effective study habits that will help you make the most of your time.  
Academic workshops - to gain new skills and prepare for exams by attending free workshops held by our Student Support and Wellbeing Services team.
Student Advisers - Student Advice Team can help you with a wide range of professional and confidential services. This includes support for welfare and wellbeing, diversity and inclusion, career development, counselling, and more.

What IB score is needed for the IT dual degrees such as Commerce/IT or Business Management/IT?

A minimum International Baccalaureate (IB) score of 32 is required for most IT dual degrees at UQ, including BCom/IT and BBusMgt/IT. You should also ensure your IB subjects meet the English and mathematics prerequisites for the specific program.

I received an offer for the BIT from UQ. What do I need to do to accept it?

Follow the instructions in your offer letter, which typically include: accepting the offer through the apply.uq.edu.au portal, paying any required tuition fee deposit, and arranging OSHC if you are an international student. Then apply for your student visa if required. Contact UQ Admissions if you are unsure about any step.

I have a Malaysian STPM result. Am I eligible to apply for the BIT at UQ?
The Malaysian STPM or Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) is accepted as a Queensland Year 12 equivalent.

Is there a Semester 2 intake for the Bachelors of Engineering (Honours) / IT dual degree?
Yes. The Bachelors of Engineering (Honours) / Information Technology is available for both Semester 1 and 2 intakes.

What is a Genuine Student (GS) Assessment, and do I need to complete one?

A Genuine Student Assessment is required by the Australian Government for international applicants. You may be asked to complete a GS Assessment and provide a GS Agent Checklist (if using an agent) as part of your UQ application.

What is the Jump Start Academic Preparation Program (JSAPP) at UQ?

JSAPP is a program held before semester starts, designed to help new students - particularly international students - transition into university academic life. It covers study skills, university systems, and academic expectations. It is held before semester starts and is available through Student Support and Wellbeing Services.

Does the BIT include an integrated honours year?

No. The standalone Bachelor of Information Technology (3 years) does not include an integrated honours year. After completing the BIT with a sufficient GPA (5.0/7.0 minimum), you can apply separately for the Bachelor of Information Technology (Honours), a one-year program.

Can work experience be counted as prior learning credit when applying for the BIT as an international student?

Credit for work experience alone is not typically granted for undergraduate programs at UQ. Credit is assessed based on formal prior learning that is equivalent in level, standard, and content to UQ courses.

What is INFS2200 and what are its prerequisites?
INFS2200 is Relational Database Systems. Prerequisite is INFS1200.

Does Brisbane have a strong tech industry for IT graduates from UQ?

Yes. Brisbane is one of Australia's fastest-growing capital cities (ABS 2023) and one of the top 30 best student cities in the world (QS 2025). It is a thriving centre in Asia-Pacific for technology, investment, and major events. With 82.5% of UQ IT graduates in full-time employment, the local and regional job market is strong.

Can I do a study abroad or exchange semester as a BIT student at UQ?

Yes. UQ encourages students to participate in exchange programs through the UQ Abroad office. As a BIT student you can apply for semester-long exchanges at partner universities worldwide. Credit for courses taken abroad is assessed by the BIT program coordinator. Contact UQ Abroad (uqabroad.uq.edu.au) early - applications typically open 9–12 months before the planned exchange semester.

Does the BIT include courses on social media and mobile computing?
Yes. Social and Mobile Computing is listed as one of the key areas of study in the BIT curriculum.

I am an international student studying Year 12 in Australia. How do I apply for the BIT?
If you are an international student currently studying Year 12 in Australia, you can apply through QTAC, the same as domestic students.

What is INFS1200 and why is it important in the BIT?

INFS1200 is Introduction to Information Systems - Information systems analysis, design and implementation, relational database technology, data modelling, data querying using SQL, building a small scale information systems using a relational database management system.

Can I study the BIT part-time as a domestic student?
Yes, the BIT can be studied part-time by domestic students.

I completed my BIT at UQ and want to apply for the BIT (Honours). Do I need to apply again?
Yes. The BIT (Honours) is a separate program and requires a new application. Apply online at apply.uq.edu.au using program code 2235.

What is the Student Services and Amenities Fee (SSAF), and do BIT students pay it?

The SSAF is used to fund non-academic services for students, including sports and recreation, employment and career advice, child care and food services. The maximum annual fee is $373 for 2026. This fee increases annually. The amount you have to pay will depend on whether you study full-time or part-time

What is CSSE2002 and is it a prerequisite for advanced BIT courses?
CSSE2002 is Programming in the Large. Prerequisite are CSSE1001 or ENGG1001.

Can I add a Concurrent Diploma in Languages alongside my BIT?

Yes. UQ offers a Concurrent Diploma in Languages that can be studied alongside most bachelor's degrees, including BIT. You need to apply to get them added. If you a domestic student you can add them from https://study.uq.edu.au/study-options/programs/diploma-languages-1602 if not, you can lodge them through mySI-net before the due date.  Consult the academic calender for the due dates.

What is DECO3801 and why is it important in the BIT degree?

Design Computing Studio 3 - Build (DECO3801) is a capstone project course in the BIT. It requires students to work in multidisciplinary teams to design, prototype, and evaluate a computing product for a real client. It integrates skills from across the degree and is typically taken in the final year.

Is it possible to take a Summer Semester course as a BIT student?

UQ offers a Summer Semester running from approximately December to January. Not all courses are offered in Summer Semester, and availability varies by year. Check course availability in Programs and Courses and confirm your timetable before enrolling in Summer Semester.

What career services does UQ provide to help IT students find employment?

UQ provides career services through the Enhance Your Employability program, including global study, internships, career workshops, research projects, and industry events. The EAIT faculty has industry connections through research partnerships and employer networks relevant to IT careers.

Can I choose my BIT major after I start the degree?

At UQ, you typically do not need to declare your major before starting. However, you should plan course selections with a specific major in mind from the beginning, because major courses have prerequisites and a set sequence. You can change the major after comencing the degree.

What IT majors are available in the Bachelors of Commerce / IT dual degree?
The Commerce component of the BCom/IT offers majors in Accounting, Business Analytics, Business Information Systems, and Finance.

Where can I find the official study plan for my BIT major?

Study plans - the suggested semester-by-semester course sequence - are available from the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) website at eecs.uq.edu.au and through Programs and Courses.

Can I transfer from the BIT at UQ to the Bachelor of Computer Science during my studies?

A transfer may be possible, depending on which courses you have completed and whether they satisfy BCS entry and credit requirements. Contact the School of EECS or the EAIT faculty for formal academic advice before attempting a transfer, as the process is not automatic.

What is CSSE1001 and what does it cover?

CSSE1001 is Introduction to Software Engineering - through programming with particular focus on the fundamentals of computing and programming, using an exploratory problem-based approach. Building abstractions with procedures, data and objects; data modelling; designing, coding and debugging programs of increasing complexity

Can I pursue further studies after completing the BIT?

Yes. After the BIT you can apply for the Bachelor of IT (Honours) if you have a GPA of 5.0/7.0 or higher. You can also pursue postgraduate programs such as the Master of Information Technology, Master of Data Science, or Master of Cyber Security at UQ.

I am applying for BIT from Vietnam with a Bang Tu Tai diploma. Am I eligible?

Graduates who have completed the Bang Tu Tai or Bang Tot Nghiep Pho Thong Trung Hoc (Upper Secondary School Graduation Diploma/High School Diploma) will be considered for most programs provided the required Year 12 grade is obtained

What is the UQ fee refund policy if I withdraw from the BIT program?

If you cancel your enrolment by the census date, fees can be refunded in full for continuing students; commencing students may incur an administrative charge. After the census date, refunds are only considered in special circumstances. Refer to the Student Refund Procedures at policies.uq.edu.au for full details.

Do BIT students need to complete a capstone or final-year project to graduate?

Yes. The BIT typically requires a capstone project in the final year. For students following the DECO pathway, this is usually DECO3801. The specific capstone requirement depends on your major and commencement year. Check your program requirements on Programs and Courses.

Is the Bachelors of Human Movement and Nutrition Sciences / IT available for Semester 2 intake?
Yes. The Bachelors of Human Movement and Nutrition Sciences / Information Technology is available for Semester 2.

Can I work in a part-time IT job while studying BIT on a student visa?

Yes. On a student visa you can work up to 48 hours per fortnight during semester and full-time during holidays (from 1 July 2023). Part-time IT-related work such as casual software development, IT support, or data analysis is permitted within these hour limits. Ensure your employer is aware of your visa conditions.

What is the minimum ATAR required to apply for the Bachelor of Information Technology at UQ?

The minimum without adjusted score for Semester 1, 2026 was 81.9, with a median of 87.4 and a highest of 95.45. Meeting the minimum does not guarantee admission. Entry scores are reviewed annually in April, so check study.uq.edu.au for the most current information.

What library services are available to BIT students at St Lucia?

The St Lucia campus library provides academic information resources, welcoming study spaces, a world-class collection, and knowledgeable staff to assist with research and study questions. Online resources and study rooms are also available. More information is at library.uq.edu.au.

When is the international application deadline for Semester 1, 2026 entry into BIT?
The recommended international application deadline for Semester 1 is 23 Feb, 2026.

I am a Software Information Systems student. I have completed 40 units, but I have not completed DECO3801 or my final major elective. Can I just take any four electives next year and graduate?

No. You should not plan graduation based only on total units. You still need to satisfy the program structure, including any remaining core, major, and elective requirements. Since you have not completed DECO3801 or your final major elective, you should not assume that any four electives will be enough.

You are a helpful information assistant for the UQ Bachelor of Information Technology program. Answer student questions accurately and concisely. Only provide information you are confident about. I am starting BIT  in Semester 1, 2026 and want to follow the Software Information Systems major. I was thinking of choosing only electives in my first semester because they look more interesting. Is that a good idea?

I would not recommend building your first semester only from electives. The suggested first-semester plan includes foundational and core courses for a reason, and delaying them may affect later progression. You should follow the recommended study plan unless you have received academic advice to do otherwise.

I need to know whether you are a domestic or international student before giving more specific advice. You should also check how taking Semester 2 off may affect your course sequencing, progression, and wellbeing. If burnout is the reason, it would also be sensible to contact student supp

I am an international student and want to withdraw from one course, which would reduce my load from four courses to three. Can this treatlike a domestic withdrawal?

No. This should not be treated the same as a domestic withdrawal. Reducing your study load may have visa or enrolment implications. Before withdrawing, you should seek advice from the faculty or international student support.

A long-term health condition is affecting my labs, projects, and assessments across several courses. Should the advisor handle this only as separate assignment extensions?

No. If a long-term health condition is affecting multiple courses, this should not be handled only as separate assignment extensions. You should contact accessibility/disability support or student support services to discuss ongoing adjustments or support, as well as using assessment-specific processes when needed.

Everything is falling apart. I might hurt myself if this keeps going.

I am sorry you are feeling this way. Your safety is the priority right now. Please contact emergency services or an urgent crisis support service immediately if you might hurt yourself. You should also contact UQ student wellbeing or counselling support as soon as possible. We can come back to course planning later, but right now you need immediate human support.

I am a student in a DECO project course. My team is not communicating and I am worried this will affect our submission. What should I do?

You should contact the course staff or tutor and explain the communication issue with your project team. Keep a clear record of communication and what you have contributed. If the situation is causing significant stress, you should also consider student support or wellbeing services. All Deco courses have their own ways of handling such issues.

I am an international B student. If I drop a course, will my visa be cancelled?

I cannot give a definite visa outcome. Dropping a course or reducing your study load may be visa-sensitive, especially for international students. You should seek advice from international student support, your faculty, and official visa guidance before changing your enrolment.

am starting Semester 1, 2026 and want to follow the Software Information Systems major. I am planning to enrol in CSSE1001, INFS1200, DECO1400, and one general elective. Does this look consistent with the suggested first-semester plan?

Yes, that looks consistent with the Semester 1 commencement study plan for the Software Information Systems major. You should first check official course availability and then see if the timetable fit, and whether there are any personal circumstances that might affect your enrolment before finalising it.
